target.title: feat(openresponses): return reasoning/thinking content in /v1/responses output
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The `/v1/responses` endpoint does not return model reasoning/thinking content. When models produce chain-of-thought reasoning (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI o-series, Gemini thinking, Ollama thinking models), the reasoning output is silently discarded by the HTTP API. Callers have no way to observe model reasoning through the OpenResponses interface.
- **Why it matters:** The [Open Responses spec](https://www.openresponses.org/reference) defines `type: "reasoning"` as a first-class output item (`ReasoningBody`) and `response.reasoning.delta`/`response.reasoning.done` as streaming events. Not implementing these means OpenClaw's OpenResponses endpoint is incomplete relative to the spec, and API callers (dashboards, orchestrators, observability tools) cannot access reasoning data.
- **What changed:** The endpoint now captures reasoning content from agent thinking events and returns it as `type: "reasoning"` output items (non-stream) or `response.reasoning.delta`/`response.reasoning.done` SSE events (stream). When no assistant text is produced but reasoning exists, the reasoning text is used as a fallback response to avoid empty replies from thinking-only models.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No config changes required. No new dependencies. Existing behavior for non-reasoning models is unchanged. The `onReasoningStream` callback path for channel-specific reasoning delivery (Discord, Telegram, etc.) is unmodified. This change only adds reasoning to the HTTP API surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #54496
- Related #44513
- Related #8364
- Partially addresses #31449 (OpenResponses reasoning output/events only; full tool I/O/context parity still pending)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A — this is a new feature implementing an existing spec item.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### Non-streaming responses

When reasoning is produced, the response `output` array now includes a `type: "reasoning"` item before the assistant message:

```json
{
  "output": [
    {
      "type": "reasoning",
      "id": "reasoning_abc123",
      "content": "Let me think about this step by step..."
    },
    {
      "type": "message",
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": [{ "type": "output_text", "text": "The answer is 42." }]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Streaming responses

Two new SSE event types are emitted during streaming:

- `response.reasoning.delta` — cumulative reasoning text as it streams
- `response.reasoning.done` — final reasoning text when complete

### Fallback behavior

When a model produces reasoning but no assistant text (common with thinking-only model configurations), the reasoning content is used as the response text instead of returning "No response from OpenClaw."

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
Model produces reasoning + answer:

  [thinking events] ──→ collect reasoning ──→ output[0]: reasoning item
  [assistant events] ──→ collect text     ──→ output[1]: assistant message

Model produces reasoning only (no assistant text):

  [thinking events] ──→ collect reasoning ──→ output[0]: reasoning item
  (no assistant text)                     ──→ output[1]: assistant = reasoning fallback
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No — reasoning content was already produced by the model, it was just being discarded

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.4 (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime: Node 22, Bun 1.2
- Model: any reasoning-capable model (Claude, o-series, Gemini thinking)

### Steps

1. Send a POST to `/v1/responses` with a reasoning-capable model
2. Observe the response `output` array

### Expected

Reasoning content appears as `type: "reasoning"` output item.

### Actual

Same as expected.

## Evidence

- [x] `pnpm build` green
- [x] `pnpm check` green (lint, format, types)
- [x] `src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts` — 12/12 tests pass
- [x] `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.*` — 21/30 pass (9 pre-existing upstream failures, verified same count on clean `upstream/main`)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: non-stream reasoning collection, stream reasoning events, fallback from thinking to text, cleanup on error
- Edge cases: no reasoning produced (no-op), reasoning-only response (fallback), tool-call + reasoning combined output
- Not verified: production load, all provider-specific thinking formats

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — adds new output items, does not remove or change existing ones
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reasoning content could be large (10k+ chars) for complex prompts, increasing response payload size
  - Mitigation: This mirrors the model's actual output; callers can ignore the reasoning item if not needed
- Risk: double `emitAgentEvent` calls (raw + formatted) for channel-streaming reasoning mode
  - Mitigation: The raw-only event fires first for HTTP capture; the formatted event fires only when `streamReasoning` is active. HTTP consumers filter by `rawText` presence.


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: feat(extension): support session cache for volcengine
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Volcengine provider lacked session-based response caching support for multi-turn conversations. - Why it matters: Without session caching, every turn sends full context to the API, increasing latency and cost for Volcengine Doubao models. - What changed: Added extensions/volcengine/cache.ts with session cache logic — manages previous_response_id, caching, thinking, store, and expire_at fields automatically. Configured via params.cache: { enable: true, ttlSec?, thinking? }. Wired into the provider via wrapStreamFn in index.ts. (cache.test.ts, 27 tests). Updated docs with config examples. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Provider registration, catalog, auth flow unchanged. No changes outside extensions/volcengine/ and docs. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). Once enable cache and openai-responses, we can see the cache read token from session file for each LLM request config example： ``` "models": { "providers": { "volcengine": { "api": "openai-responses" } } }, "agents": { "defaults": { "models": { "volcengine/xxxx": { "alias": "volcengine", "params": { "cache": { "enable": false, "ttlSec": 3600, "thinking": true } } } } } } ``` session file example: ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: enable cache, and check the cached read token - Edge cases checked: Once the cache token expired by set the ttl with 120s, OpenClaw will retry the LLM request and cache token agent once receive new message - What you did **not** verify: ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. target.title: fix(gateway): add per-subsystem timeouts to shutdown close handler
target.body:
## Summary

- A single hung subsystem `stop()` call (e.g. Telegram polling stuck during network recovery) can consume the entire 25 s `SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS` budget, preventing remaining subsystems from cleaning up and forcing users to `kill -9` the gateway process.
- Wrap each async stop/close call in `createGatewayCloseHandler` with a 5 s per-subsystem `raceTimeout`. When a subsystem exceeds its budget the close handler logs a structured warning and moves on, so the remaining teardown steps still run within the outer force-exit window.
- Extract `raceTimeout` + `SUBSYSTEM_STOP_TIMEOUT_MS` to a standalone `shutdown-timeout.ts` (zero external deps, independently testable).
- Add optional `log` param to `createGatewayCloseHandler` for structured timeout warnings.

## Motivation

Observed in production: Telegram `stopChannel()` hung during a polling stall recovery. The 25 s `forceExitTimer` in `run-loop.ts` fired before WebSocket and HTTP server teardown could run. `kill` (SIGTERM) did not exit; `kill -9` (SIGKILL) was required. Related: #51100, #49910.

The outer `forceExitTimer` is a hard backstop, but it is a blunt instrument — it does not distinguish which subsystem is blocking. Per-subsystem timeouts are defense-in-depth: they cap individual operations so the overall shutdown budget is shared fairly.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/gateway/shutdown-timeout.ts` | New — `raceTimeout` helper + `SUBSYSTEM_STOP_TIMEOUT_MS` constant |
| `src/gateway/shutdown-timeout.test.ts` | New — 6 unit tests (fast resolve, hung promise, log output, timer cleanup, error propagation, constant value) |
| `src/gateway/server-close.ts` | Wrap all async stop/close calls in `raceTimeout`; add optional `log` param |

## Test plan

- [x] 6 unit tests for `raceTimeout` pass locally
- [ ] Existing `server-close.test.ts` unchanged (pre-existing `bundledChannelPlugins` infra issue affects all 175 gateway tests on `main` — not introduced by this PR)
- [ ] CI lint + type check pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat: include provider/model/profile/trigger context in overloaded and rate limit error messages
target.body:
## Problem

Transient provider failures were still too terse to act on. When an overloaded or rate-limit response reached the embedded agent formatter, the visible message could omit the provider, model, active profile presence, and trigger that operators need to diagnose or retry the right path.

## Solution

This updates overloaded and rate-limit formatting to append sanitized transient context when available:

- provider/model, for example `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `profile=active` when an auth profile was selected
- `trigger=...`

Provider/model and trigger remain readable. Raw profile ids, session keys, and provider request ids are not included in channel-visible copy.

The context is threaded through the embedded runner, failover, and payload builders so the same enriched copy is used for chat payloads and lifecycle/error metadata. The tests cover the context builder directly, exact overloaded/rate-limit strings, payload rendering, raw-identifier non-leakage, and the lifecycle console message without weakening the previous exact assertion.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Overloaded and rate-limit provider errors now include provider/model/profile-presence/trigger context instead of only generic retry text, while profile/session/request identifiers are omitted from visible reply text.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `9f4f5b2e2b8ae9e900b620c1013ae37fae920d88` on Node 22, executing the production embedded-agent formatter from source.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx --eval 'import { formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts"; ...'` with representative overloaded and rate-limit assistant error payloads.
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the local OpenClaw command:

```text
overloaded: The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment. [anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, profile=active, trigger=heartbeat]
rate-limit: ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later. [openai/gpt-5.4, profile=active, trigger=cron]
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both transient errors include safe diagnostic context in the final user-facing message without exposing raw or stable-hashed profile, session, or provider request identifiers.
- **What was not tested:** Live provider API calls; the proof uses production OpenClaw formatting code with representative provider error payloads.

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`

## Related issues

Closes #43932
Closes #51336
Closes #30262

I did not run a live channel repro in this read-only review, but Mantis proof demonstrates the candidate behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This is a narrow repair candidate because the useful logic can be moved to the active Markdown core renderer with focused regression coverage and no product decision.

target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.

target.title: Rescue: add watchdog core service and cron engine
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had no built-in isolated rescue watchdog that could detect an unhealthy managed gateway profile and repair it without going through the failing primary session.
- Why it matters: the larger rescue-watchdog feature is hard to review as one PR; this split lands the core runtime/service layer first so maintainers can review the actual watchdog mechanism without onboarding/UI noise.
- What changed: adds daemon/service hardening needed by the watchdog, introduces a new isolated `rescueWatchdog` cron payload + runner, and wires the gateway cron service to execute that watchdog job against a monitored profile.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no onboarding wizard flow, no CLI `openclaw onboard --rescue-watchdog` UX, and no docs changes in this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #31480
- Supersedes #44113
- Supersedes #46493
- Supersedes #46499

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Adds a new internal isolated cron payload kind, `rescueWatchdog`, for watchdog jobs that monitor a target profile and repair it when unhealthy.
- Hardens LaunchAgent plist writes: trusted-home resolution, symlink rejection, atomic writes, and `0600` plist permissions.
- Managed service restart helpers now accept `AbortSignal`, so watchdog restart attempts can be canceled cleanly instead of hanging until command timeouts.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  The new capability is intentionally narrow: an isolated cron job can probe and repair a monitored local profile. Risk is bounded by restricting the payload shape, disallowing delivery targets for `rescueWatchdog`, resolving the monitored profile explicitly, and hardening the underlying managed-service restart and launchd plist write paths. The repair fallback uses exact argv via `process.execPath` and avoids shell-based PATH resolution.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): local managed gateway service / cron service only

### Steps

1. Run the daemon/unit tests covering launchd, systemd, schtasks, and process-exec timeout behavior.
2. Run the cron/rescue unit tests covering payload normalization, job validation, watchdog execution, and gateway cron wiring.
3. Build the repo and validate config to confirm the combined core layer compiles and loads cleanly.

### Expected

- A `rescueWatchdog` cron job can be normalized, stored, validated, and executed as an isolated job.
- Managed service restart helpers are abortable and launchd plist writes fail closed on unsafe targets.

### Actual

- Verified by targeted tests and local build/config validation; behavior matches the expected outcomes above.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - launchd plist path resolution ignores a conflicting `HOME` override, rejects symlink targets, and writes plist files with `0600` permissions via temp-file + rename.
  - `AbortSignal` now propagates through launchd/systemd/schtasks restart helpers into the process exec layer.
  - `rescueWatchdog` cron payloads normalize correctly, are forced to isolated session targets, and reject delivery/failureDestination config.
  - the rescue watchdog runner probes the monitored profile, attempts managed service restart, falls back to `doctor --repair --non-interactive`, and reports success/error summaries correctly.
- Edge cases checked:
  - `os.userInfo()` failure falls back to `os.homedir()`, and unresolved trusted home fails closed.
  - monitored rescue-profile names are rejected.
  - restart/repair flows respect cron timeout budget and abort behavior.
  - `launchd` temp plist files are cleaned up if rename fails.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - manual end-to-end watchdog repair on a real long-running host with an actually failing managed gateway.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/daemon/*`, `src/process/exec.ts`, `src/cron/*`, `src/rescue/watchdog-shared.ts`, `src/gateway/server-cron.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: launchd install unexpectedly failing on valid paths, or isolated cron jobs rejecting valid `agentTurn` jobs after the new payload kind was added.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: LaunchAgent path hardening could reject previously tolerated but unsafe filesystem layouts.
  - Mitigation: the checks are limited to symlinked/non-directory targets in the LaunchAgents path and have focused test coverage.
- Risk: adding a new cron payload kind could accidentally loosen validation or delivery behavior.
  - Mitigation: `rescueWatchdog` is explicitly restricted to isolated jobs, delivery is rejected, and schema/service/normalization paths have dedicated tests.
- Risk: watchdog repair code could leave stuck restart attempts behind.
  - Mitigation: abort propagation is threaded through the restart backends and the watchdog enforces bounded restart/repair timeouts.

target.title: fix(acpx): fail closed on Windows wrapper spawns
target.body:
Closes #93465.

Summary:
- avoid direct Windows .cmd/.bat/.ps1 adapter wrapper spawns from ACPX generated launchers
- report ACP session-init spawn failures with redacted gateway logging and actionable Windows guidance
- keep the touched extension check green by making bounded Codex thread config merging total
- refresh generated Codex prompt snapshots and current guard baselines for the rebased main

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: ACPX launch preparation rejects unresolved Windows PowerShell wrappers and generated Claude ACP launchers no longer contain the direct `npx.cmd` fallback that produced raw `spawn EINVAL` failures.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the real plugin SDK Windows spawn resolver and ACPX generated-wrapper path with `platform: "win32"` inputs.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` created a temporary `npx.ps1`, called `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram` with Windows platform/PATHEXT inputs, then generated the Claude ACP wrapper with `prepareAcpxCodexAuthConfig`.

Evidence after fix: Console output from the live command:

```json
{
  "ps1WrapperRejected": true,
  "generatedWrapperHasWin32Guard": true,
  "generatedWrapperMentionsDisabledDirectNpx": true,
  "generatedWrapperDirectNpxCmdLiteral": false
}
```

Observed result after fix: The PowerShell wrapper path failed closed, and the generated launcher includes the Windows guard without a direct `npx.cmd` literal fallback.

What was not tested: Live Windows gateway launch was not tested on this macOS host.

target.title: Backup: add encrypted snapshot backup flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had local backup export/verify, but no low-friction folder-backed snapshot flow for encrypted backups plus workspace mirroring.
- Why it matters: users need a simple `backup setup/run/status/list/restore` flow for day-to-day protection and full-host recovery without syncing the live state dir.
- What changed: added folder-backed encrypted snapshot storage, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore/list plumbing, config schema/docs, and the follow-up hardening/regression coverage that addressed the P1/P2 review feedback from #41345.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add provider-specific cloud APIs, device-identity export/import, or any live state-dir sync behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #32921
- Related #40090
- Related #7598
- Related #41345

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Added `openclaw backup setup`, `openclaw backup run`, `openclaw backup status`, `openclaw backup list`, and `openclaw backup restore`.
- `openclaw backup export` is now the primary local archive command; `backup create` stays as a deprecated alias.
- `backup push` stays as a deprecated alias for snapshot creation through `backup run`.
- `reset` and `uninstall` now recommend `openclaw backup export`.
- Backup config now supports `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key` for folder-backed encrypted snapshots.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Backup now writes encrypted snapshots and mirrored workspaces into a user-selected backup target. Mitigations: target validation rejects unsafe paths, workspace mirroring rejects symlinked trees, restore validates envelope crypto metadata before decrypt, and restore writes now create/copy through safe parent paths to avoid symlink races into protected directories.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `backup.target=<temp dir>`, `backup.encryption.key=<redacted>`

### Steps

1. Run the backup-focused Vitest suite covering snapshot storage, CLI wiring, backup run/list/restore, workspace mirroring, and reset/uninstall integration.
2. Run `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` on the rebased branch.
3. Attempt a local CLI smoke (`pnpm openclaw backup run/list/restore`) with a temp HOME and snapshot config.

### Expected

- Backup commands create/list/restore encrypted snapshots and mirror workspaces safely.
- Build/check pass unless blocked by unrelated main-branch issues.
- Local CLI smoke exercises the new flow end-to-end.

### Actual

- The backup-focused Vitest suite passed.
- `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, and the local CLI smoke are currently blocked by unrelated `main` breakage after rebasing: `@mariozechner/pi-ai` no longer exports `getOAuthApiKey`, `getOAuthProviders`, or `loginOpenAICodex`, which breaks `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.ts`, `src/commands/openai-codex-oauth.ts`, and several agent wrapper files before backup code runs.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/backup.test.ts src/commands/backup-run.test.ts src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup-status.test.ts src/commands/backup-snapshot.test.ts src/commands/backup-restore.test.ts src/commands/workspace-backup.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/config.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/encryption.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/provider-folder.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/targets.test.ts src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts src/cli/program/command-registry.test.ts
pnpm build
pnpm check
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup run --json
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup list --json
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup restore <snapshotId> --mode workspace-only --json
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: backup-focused test suite on the rebased branch, including snapshot create/list/restore, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore mapping, rollback handling, JSON output, and the added envelope/symlink hardening regressions.
- Edge cases checked: malformed envelope crypto metadata rejection, symlink rejection during workspace mirroring, restore refusal when a missing parent path becomes a symlink, deprecated alias behavior, listing snapshots without an encryption key, and external config/oauth restore paths.
- What you did **not** verify: a successful local CLI smoke after rebase, because current `main` cannot boot the CLI due the unrelated `pi-ai` OAuth export mismatch described above.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Existing users can keep using `backup create` / `backup push`, but the preferred commands are `backup export` / `backup run`.
  - To enable encrypted snapshots, set `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key`.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: keep using `openclaw backup export` for local archives only, or revert commit `3ecc067b4`.
- Files/config to restore: `openclaw.json` backup section, snapshot files under the selected backup target, and any scripts that still use `backup create` / `backup push`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `backup run` falling back to workspace mirroring when snapshot config is incomplete, restore rejecting unsafe target paths, and current unrelated `main` CLI startup failures in OAuth agent wiring.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: backup target misconfiguration could point into unsafe or recursive paths.
  - Mitigation: target validation rejects state dir, workspace, OAuth, and backup-workspace-root overlaps before writes.
- Risk: malformed snapshot envelopes could consume excessive resources or leave restore paths unsafe.
  - Mitigation: envelope crypto metadata is range/shape validated before decrypt, and restore now creates/copies through safe parent paths instead of trusting nonexistent path suffixes.
- Risk: alias compatibility could drift from the primary commands.
  - Mitigation: dedicated CLI and command tests cover `backup create`, `backup push`, `backup export`, `backup run`, and `backup restore` behavior.

target.title: feat(line): add optional requireMentionForNonText group gate
target.body:
## Summary

When `requireMention: true` is set on a LINE group, text messages are correctly gated by `resolveInboundMentionDecision`, but non-text messages (image, sticker, video, audio, file, location) always bypass the gate via `canDetectMention: message.type === "text"`. The LINE Messaging API never delivers `mention.mentionees` on non-text messages, so a bot cannot be @-mentioned on an image in a group, yet the handler still calls `processMessage` for every piece of unaddressed media. In busy groups this leads to the agent processing large volumes of images/stickers/videos that were never directed at it.

This PR adds an opt-in `requireMentionForNonText?: boolean` to `LineGroupConfig` (default `false`). When enabled, non-text messages participate in the same mention gate as text, and because LINE does not expose a native bot mention on those messages they are skipped under `requireMention` — matching how WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord/Slack groups behave: unaddressed media does not trigger a reply.

Because the default is `false`, the historical fail-open behavior is fully preserved. The existing test ``allows non-text group messages through when requireMention is set (cannot detect mention)`` continues to pass unchanged.

## Why an opt-in and not a default change

Flipping the default would be a behavior change for every deployment that currently relies on non-text group media reaching the bot (e.g. groups that send images for the agent to analyze without explicit @-mention). Keeping it opt-in preserves that flow while giving operators a one-line knob to bring LINE in line with other channels.

## Example config

\`\`\`jsonc
{
  "channels": {
    "line": {
      "accounts": {
        "default": {
          "groups": {
            "G0123456789abcdef": {
              "requireMention": true,
              "requireMentionForNonText": true
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test:extension line` — **235/235 passing**, including two new tests:
  - `skips non-text group messages when requireMentionForNonText is set`
  - `still processes text group messages with bot mention when requireMentionForNonText is set`
- [x] Existing `allows non-text group messages through when requireMention is set (cannot detect mention)` left untouched and still passes (default unchanged).
- [x] No shared plugin-sdk / contract surfaces touched — only files under `extensions/line/src/`.

## AI-assistance disclosure

- [x] AI-assisted (Cursor + Claude). Marked per CONTRIBUTING.md.
- [x] Fully tested locally via `pnpm test:extension line` before opening this PR.
- [x] I understand what the code does: the change threads a new optional group-config field into the existing mention-gate call in `bot-handlers.ts` and wires it through the zod schema + TS type; the shared mention-decision helper is unchanged.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: feat: configure metadata (contextWindow, maxTokens, etc.) for custom provider setup
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets: - Problem: `openclaw configure --section model` sets up providers and auth but never prompts for model metadata (contextWindow, maxTokens, reasoning, input types, cost). Users must hand-edit config or run many `config set` calls to configure these. - Why it matters: Custom/self-hosted endpoints default to contextWindow=16000 and maxTokens=4096, which are often wrong. Users have no interactive way to correct this during setup. - What changed: After custom provider setup in `promptAuthConfig`, the wizard now asks "Configure metadata for `/`?" and walks through the core `ModelDefinitionConfig` fields (identity, capabilities, limits, cost) with current values prefilled. Built-in providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) are unaffected. Their catalog already provides defaults. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): The existing provider/auth flow is untouched. The metadata prompt is purely additive (appended after the custom provider path only). No config schema changes. No new CLI commands. Declining the prompt preserves the previous behavior exactly. Built-in provider flows are not modified. Compat flags (supportsStore, supportsTools, etc.) are not prompted for; they use sensible runtime defaults when absent and can be set manually in config by advanced users. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `openclaw configure --section model` with a **custom provider**: after provider setup completes, a new confirm prompt appears: "Configure metadata for `/`? (context window, max tokens, etc.)". Answering Yes walks through all model metadata fields. Answering No preserves previous behavior. - **Built-in providers** (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, etc.): no change. The metadata prompt does not appear. - Fields prompted (custom only): display name, API adapter, reasoning, input modalities, context window, max output tokens, token pricing (gated behind its own confirm). - All fields prefilled with current values; pressing Enter keeps defaults. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin 25.2.0) - Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm - Model/provider: Custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint ([thegrid.ai](https://thegrid.ai/)) - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): Standard `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with custom provider ### Steps 1. Run `pnpm openclaw configure --section model` 2. Select "Custom Provider" and complete auth/model setup 3. Observe the new prompt: "Configure metadata for `/`? (context window, max tokens, etc.)" 4. Answer Yes and walk through the fields, or answer No to skip 5. (Optional) Repeat with a built-in provider (e.g. OpenAI) and confirm the metadata prompt does **not** appear ### Expected - After custom provider setup, a metadata configuration prompt appears - Answering Yes allows editing all core `ModelDefinitionConfig` fields - Answering No skips metadata editing (same behavior as before this change) - Updated metadata is written to `models.providers..models[]` in config - Built-in providers skip the metadata prompt entirely ### Actual - Works as expected ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) 18 unit tests in `src/commands/model-metadata.test.ts` covering: `collectConfiguredModels`, `findModelInConfig`, `findPrimaryModelEntry`, `applyModelDefinitionUpdate`, `formatTokenCount`, `promptModelMetadata`, `promptModelMetadataForPrimary` (including skip for built-in providers). All passing. Existing `configure.wizard.test.ts` tests also pass. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Ran `pnpm openclaw configure --section model` end-to-end with a custom provider (thegrid-ai), confirmed metadata prompt appears after model setup. Ran with OpenAI, confirmed metadata prompt does **not** appear. - Edge cases checked: Declining metadata prompt preserves config unchanged; missing primary model gracefully skips; provider with no models returns empty list; built-in providers silently skip the prompt - What you did **not** verify: All built-in provider auth paths (only tested OpenAI). Other built-in providers use the same `authChoice !== "custom-api-key"` code path so are expected to behave identically. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes`: declining the new prompt produces identical config to before - Config/env changes? `No`: writes to existing `models.providers.*.models[]` fields - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the three-line addition in `configure.gateway-auth.ts` (lines 138-140: the `if (authChoice === "custom-api-key")` block calling `promptModelMetadataForPrimary`) - Files/config to restore: `src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.ts` (remove import + call), delete `src/commands/model-metadata.ts` and `src/commands/model-metadata.test.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: If the metadata prompt throws, it would interrupt the configure wizard after custom provider setup but before config write. The `WizardCancelledError` path is handled by the parent wizard. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Metadata prompt adds extra steps to the custom provider configure flow, potentially annoying for users who don't need it. - Mitigation: Gated behind a confirm ("Configure metadata?") defaulting to Yes but skippable with a single keypress. Cost is further gated behind its own confirm.

target.title: fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `event.messages` was documented as a generic user-reply channel, but only `/new` and `/reset` actually drained those messages.
- Why it matters: `message:received` hooks could push reply text that was silently dropped, while other lifecycle hooks looked supported even though they had no safe reply target.
- What changed: added a shared internal hook reply-drain helper, kept reset/new command behavior, and now deliver `message:received` hook replies when a routable reply target exists.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not make bootstrap, preprocessed/transcribed, sent, or other lifecycle-only hooks auto-reply.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor

- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39688
- Related #40185

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Internal hook replies pushed to `event.messages` are now delivered on `message:received` when OpenClaw has a reply-capable routing target.
- Docs now explicitly limit `event.messages` auto-delivery to reply-capable surfaces instead of implying it works for all hooks.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): internal hooks / inbound reply routing
- Relevant config (redacted): default hook + routeReply test harnesses

### Steps

1. Register an internal hook that pushes text into `event.messages`.
2. Trigger it from `/new` or `message:received`.
3. Verify the reply is routed only when a safe reply target exists.

### Expected

- Reply-capable surfaces route the accumulated hook messages.
- Lifecycle-only surfaces remain non-replying unless they gain an explicit delivery contract later.

### Actual

- Before this fix, `message:received` hook replies were silently dropped.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `commands-core` still routes reset/new hook replies; `dispatch-from-config` now routes `message:received` hook replies on routable surfaces and skips non-routable ones.
- Edge cases checked: non-routable surfaces do not auto-send hook replies; docs/comments now match that contract.
- What you did **not** verify: live messaging-provider delivery outside the existing routeReply test harnesses.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert `fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces`
- Files/config to restore: `src/auto-reply/reply/internal-hook-replies.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-core.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`, `docs/automation/hooks.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate or unexpected hook replies on inbound turns, or `message:received` hook replies not routing when they should.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `message:received` hook replies use the same route-reply path as other provider-routed responses, so a bad target selection would misroute the hook message.
  - Mitigation: the helper uses existing inbound routing fields (`OriginatingTo`, `To`, channel normalization) and adds explicit tests for routable and non-routable surfaces.

target.title: Fix dashboard chat run recovery across reconnects
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two closely related Gateway Dashboard chat reliability problems:

1. Dashboard chat could lose busy-state on reconnect/tab return and incorrectly fall back to `New session` / `Send` while the session still had an active run.
2. Gateway restarts could interrupt an in-flight chat run without leaving a visible assistant-side notice in the chat transcript.

## What changed

- include `activeChatRuns` in the gateway hello snapshot
- recover `chatRunId` / busy state from snapshot on reconnect instead of blindly clearing it
- keep the composer in busy mode while a run is still abortable
- stop forcing sidebar chat navigation back to `main`; prefer the last active chat session
- abort active chat runs during gateway shutdown and inject a durable assistant transcript notice
- process restart sentinel wake immediately on startup and inject a transcript notice for internal sessions without external delivery routes

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144 pnpm -s tsc --noEmit --pretty false`

## Related

- Closes #41064
- Related: #38836
- Related: #21641
- Related: #26986

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: Security: add configurable unpaired DM responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: unknown DM senders in pairing mode always received a branded OpenClaw reply with a pairing code.
- Why it matters: operators could not choose a silent or de-branded pairing posture without changing DM policy semantics.
- What changed: added `unpairedResponse` config support (`silent | code-only | branded`) and wired it through the shared pairing helpers and core DM pairing flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior remains `branded`, and non-pairing DM policies are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #29945
- Related #29945

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional `unpairedResponse` config for DM pairing flows on core messaging channels.
- `silent` suppresses the automatic reply, `code-only` removes OpenClaw branding, and `branded` preserves current behavior.
- Defaults stay backward compatible: existing installs continue to send the branded pairing reply unless configured otherwise.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Slack, Signal, iMessage, LINE, plugin runtime pairing callers
- Relevant config (redacted): default config plus `unpairedResponse` overrides in channel config

### Steps

1. Configure a DM channel with `dmPolicy: pairing`.
2. Send a DM from an unapproved sender.
3. Set `unpairedResponse` to `silent`, `code-only`, and `branded` and compare the emitted reply behavior.

### Expected

- Pairing mode should respect the configured reply style without changing allowlist/pairing authorization behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, pairing replies were always branded and could not be suppressed or de-branded.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: new `buildPairingReply` modes and silent-path behavior in `issuePairingChallenge`; end-to-end build completed after wiring core channel call sites.
- Edge cases checked: silent mode skips outbound reply send; code-only mode still includes the pairing code and approval command; branded mode remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live channel delivery against external services.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `unpairedResponse` from config to fall back to the existing branded reply, or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: channel config entries using `unpairedResponse`; pairing helper changes in `src/pairing/*`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: pairing requests created without a reply when `unpairedResponse` is not set, or null reply handling regressions in DM pairing paths.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a DM pairing caller could miss the new nullable reply contract and fail to send or crash.
  - Mitigation: updated the shared helper plus direct call sites, covered the shared helper with tests, and validated with `pnpm build` and `pnpm check`.

target.title: Security: harden gateway timeouts and auth store sealing
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `GatewayClient.request()` could hang indefinitely, auth stores were always plaintext at rest, and mirrored transcript writes did not re-assert restrictive permissions after append.
- Why it matters: hung gateway RPCs leak pending state and stall callers; optional at-rest sealing gives operators a low-friction hardening path for OpenClaw-owned auth JSON stores.
- What changed: added bounded gateway request timeouts, added optional `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` sealing for `auth-profiles.json` and legacy `oauth.json`, added regression coverage, and re-applied `0600` perms after mirrored transcript writes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not implement full session transcript encryption because `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` and multiple local readers still depend on raw JSONL session files.
- Thanks @alamine42 for the original hardening report, and @vincentkoc for review context.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #4954
- Related #7604

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `GatewayClient` requests now fail with `gateway request timeout for <method>` instead of hanging forever.
- When `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` is set, OpenClaw-owned auth stores are written in sealed form and require that same env var to be read.
- Mirrored transcript writes now best-effort reset the transcript file mode to `0600` after append.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  Optional auth-store sealing adds a new env-based operator hardening path. The format is backward compatible because plaintext reads still work when sealing is not enabled, and encrypted reads fail closed with a warning if the passphrase env var is missing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: n/a
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): optional `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE`

### Steps

1. Run the focused test suite for gateway client, auth store, oauth migration, and transcript paths.
2. Run repo-level `pnpm check`.
3. Run repo-level `pnpm build`.

### Expected

- Gateway requests time out cleanly.
- Auth stores round-trip in sealed form when `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` is set.
- Build and lint stay green.

### Actual

- Verified locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused Vitest coverage for gateway timeouts, sealed auth-store save/load, sealed oauth migration, and transcript append behavior.
- Edge cases checked: timeout cleanup removes pending entries; encrypted auth stores do not contain plaintext secrets; legacy oauth migration still works when the source file is sealed.
- What you did **not** verify: full transcript encryption, because it is explicitly out of scope for this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  Operators who want auth-store sealing can set `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` before writing credentials and keep it set for future reads.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: unset `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` before writing new auth store files, or revert the branch.
- Files/config to restore: `auth-profiles.json` / `oauth.json` from backup if an operator loses the passphrase.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: warning that an encrypted auth store requires `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` when the env var is missing.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator enables auth-store sealing and later starts OpenClaw without `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE`.
  - Mitigation: reads fail closed with a targeted warning instead of silently parsing garbage.
- Risk: timeout defaults could surface latent long-running RPC assumptions.
  - Mitigation: timeout is bounded but configurable per client/request.

target.title: feat(hooks): emit session prune lifecycle event
target.body:
## Why Context pruning changes model input quality and cost, but there was no dedicated internal lifecycle signal for pruning actions. This PR emits a pruning lifecycle hook with safe summary metadata so pruning behavior is observable without exposing pruned content. Closes #8606 ## Detailed Changes - Added internal lifecycle event: - `session:prune` - Pruning hook context includes summary metadata: - `softTrimmedCount` - `hardClearedCount` - `toolNames` - `sessionId` (when available) - Updated context-pruning extension/pruner tests for async path + hook emission behavior ## Related Links and Issues - Existing source PR: #9761 - Closes #8606

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extension’s `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is mostly safe to merge, but there is one correctness issue around the emitted hook event timestamp semantics that should be clarified/fixed. - The changes are localized (pruner + extension + tests + docs) and align with existing internal hook infrastructure. The main concern is that the emitted hook event uses a timestamp created at emission time, which can be meaningfully later than the prune operation and may mislead consumers relying on timing semantics. - src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.ts

target.title: fix: route deleted-agent session purge through lifecycle seam
target.body:
## What
Routes deleted-agent session-entry purge through a storage-boundary operation instead of a raw whole-store writer callback.

## Why
PR #93704 moved cleanup/reaper entry lifecycle mutations behind `applySessionEntryLifecycleMutation(...)`, but `purgeAgentSessionStoreEntries(...)` still owned a direct `updateSessionStore(...)` delete loop for `agents.delete`. This follow-up closes that remaining transaction-boundary gap while preserving deleted-agent behavior.

## Changes
- Add locked deleted-agent purge operation
- Preserve current-store owner scan semantics
- Preserve delete file trashing
- Extend writer boundary ratchet
- Add purge regression tests

## Tracking
- Pebbles: `clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.34`
- Branch: `clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.36/31b-agent-delete-session-purge-lifecycle`
- Head SHA: `ba74b683655c9c50ea2058a80835ec9debc3bcf7`
- Base: `clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.35/31b-session-cleanup-reaper-lifecycle-seam`

## Scope
Confirmed gap: `src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts` used `updateSessionStore(...)` for deleted-agent session-entry purge after #93704 introduced the lifecycle mutation seam.

This PR keeps CLI/gateway delete behavior, best-effort purge behavior, legacy default-agent key handling, shared-store preservation, store maintenance behavior, and caller-owned filesystem trashing intact.

## Exclusions
- No SQLite schema/runtime flip
- No doctor migration
- No runtime JSON fallback
- No plugin SDK change
- No transcript/archive cleanup moved into deleted-agent purge
- No maniple/worker use

## SQLite Adapter Instructions
The future SQLite adapter should implement `purgeDeletedAgentSessionEntries(...)` as a transaction-sized operation: choose deleted-agent rows from the current locked/transactional store state using the same default-agent/shared-store ownership semantics, delete those rows, and keep store maintenance semantics equivalent to the file backend. Do not reintroduce a pre-lock exact-entry removal plan or expose a broad mutable whole-store callback. Keep agent directory and session directory trashing in the CLI/gateway delete callers.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.agent-purge.test.ts src/commands/agents.delete.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agents-mutate.test.ts test/scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.mjs`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.mjs src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.agent-purge.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.ts test/scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search --thinking codex=low`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.35/31b-session-cleanup-reaper-lifecycle-seam --no-web-search --thinking codex=low`

target.title: Bug: openclaw-weixin startAccount ???,?????????? session
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix manifest-only/read-only channel account resolution so route-safe account IDs can resolve config entries whose raw provider IDs contain opaque characters like `@` or `.`.
- Reuse the shared normalized account lookup path and keep blocked object keys out of normalized fallback matches.
- Add regression coverage for both the openclaw-weixin-style raw account key and the blocked-key default-account case.

## Linked context

Addresses #93556.

- Fix classification: root-cause bug fix, scoped to external channel/plugin account config resolution.

- Maintainer-ready confidence: high. The fix is covered by focused routing and read-only plugin contract tests, plus live openclaw-weixin startup proof showing the external channel account starts and reaches monitor state.

- Root cause: manifest channel account listing normalized raw account keys for routing, but manifest channel account resolution used a different mapping contract by looking up the already-normalized route ID directly in the raw `accounts` object. A config key such as `59000514e8ad@im.bot` was listed as `59000514e8ad-im-bot`, but the resolver could not map that normalized ID back to the original raw config entry.

- Why this is root-cause fix: the resolver now uses the same normalized account ID contract as the lister, so raw provider IDs and route-safe IDs map through one resolver invariant. The shared normalized lookup also filters blocked raw keys during fallback matching, preserving the existing prototype-pollution guard at the lookup source instead of adding a downstream fallback.

- What did NOT change: no plugin manifest format, gateway startup behavior, account ID normalization rules, generated metadata, provider-specific openclaw-weixin code, or unrelated channel routing behavior changed.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a live external `openclaw-weixin` channel account whose raw provider ID normalizes to a route-safe account ID can be loaded by the Gateway, started through the channel plugin, and reported as running instead of being dropped before `startAccount`.

- Real environment tested: macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2, worktree SHA `3b8e699`, external `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.4.3`, isolated OpenClaw home, live QR login with Weixin.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  pnpm openclaw plugins install "@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.4.3" --force
  pnpm openclaw config set plugins.entries.openclaw-weixin.enabled true
  pnpm openclaw channels login --channel openclaw-weixin
  pnpm openclaw config set gateway.mode local
  pnpm openclaw config set gateway.port 18790
  pnpm dev gateway --port 18790
  pnpm openclaw channels status --probe
  pnpm test src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts src/routing/account-lookup.test.ts
  ```

- Evidence after fix: copied and sanitized terminal output from the live OpenClaw Gateway and channel probe.

  ```text
  Installed plugin: openclaw-weixin
  Restart the gateway to load plugins.

  已将此 OpenClaw 连接到微信。
  ```

  ```text
2026-06-17T01:02:11.730+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (10 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openclaw-weixin, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.5s)
2026-06-17T01:02:11.731+08:00 [gateway] starting channels and sidecars...
2026-06-17T01:02:11.766+08:00 [openclaw-weixin] [<route-safe-weixin-account-id>] starting weixin provider (https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com)

  ```

  ```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs channels status --probe

- openclaw-weixin <route-safe-weixin-account-id>: enabled, configured, running

Tip: https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli#status adds gateway health probes to status output (requires a reachable gateway).
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the live external `openclaw-weixin` plugin loaded from its installed npm package, the Gateway started channels and sidecars, the route-safe Weixin account ID entered `starting weixin provider`, the Weixin monitor started against `https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com`, and `channels status --probe` reported that same account as `enabled, configured, running`.

- What was not tested: no inbound Weixin DM was sent through to an agent reply in this proof; this live check covers QR auth, account resolution, channel startup, provider start, monitor start, and Gateway status for the fixed account path. The exact issue-reported `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.1.8` package was not used for live startup because the current OpenClaw installer rejects that npm package for missing compiled runtime output; the live startup proof used the current official 2.x package line.

## Tests and validation

- Plugin test / extension-specific contract coverage: `src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts`
- Routing helper regression coverage: `src/routing/account-lookup.test.ts`
- Validation result: `2 passed (2)` test files, `36 passed (36)` tests.

  ```text

   Duration  4.97s (transform 1.44s, setup 191ms, import 1.74s, tests 2.95s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 9.54s
  ```

## Risk checklist

- [x] No dependency, schema, migration, generated metadata, or public plugin API changes.
- [x] No local paths, credentials, or private logs are included in this PR body.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review after focused regression tests and live openclaw-weixin startup proof on the current head.
